name: google-calendar-automation
description: Automate Google Calendar events, scheduling, availability checks, and attendee management via Rube MCP (Composio). Create events, find free slots, manage attendees, and list calendars programmatically.
capabilities: Create and update Google Calendar events with ISO 8601 timestamps
Find available time slots using free/busy queries
Search and list events by date range or keywords
Add and remove attendees from existing events
List and manage multiple Google Calendars
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 125 potential issues, all of which are false positives from markdown code formatting. The skill is documentation-only (SKILL.md) with no executable code. The 'external_commands' detections are backticks used for markdown code spans like `GOOGLECALENDAR_CREATE_EVENT`. The single network reference is a legitimate MCP endpoint configuration URL. No security risks identified.
valueStatement: Manage your Google Calendar programmatically through Claude Code. Create events, check availability, manage attendees, and sync calendars without leaving your development workflow.
userTitle: Automate Google Calendar with Claude Code
limitations: Requires active Google Calendar connection via Rube MCP
Does not support natural language dates (requires ISO 8601 format)
Cannot resolve attendee names to emails (requires email addresses)
Google Meet link creation may fail on personal Gmail accounts

useCases.title: Schedule Development Sprints
useCases.description: Create recurring sprint planning events, automatically add team members, and find time slots that work for everyone.

useCases.title: Calendar-Driven Task Automation
useCases.description: Check availability before scheduling meetings, avoid conflicts, and maintain a clean calendar workflow.

useCases.title: Team Meeting Coordination
useCases.description: Find common free slots across multiple calendars, add attendees, and send meeting invitations.

promptTemplates.title: Create a Simple Event
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Google Calendar event tomorrow at 2pm PST called 'Team Standup' that lasts 30 minutes.
promptTemplates.scenario: Basic event creation with natural language description

promptTemplates.title: Find Available Time Slots
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all available time slots between 9am and 5pm tomorrow that are at least 1 hour long.

promptTemplates.title: Search and Update Events
promptTemplates.prompt: Search for events titled 'Sprint Review' and add john@example.com as an attendee.
promptTemplates.scenario: Finding events by keyword and modifying attendees

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Calendar Availability
promptTemplates.prompt: Check availability for next week across my primary calendar and work calendar, then find a 2-hour free slot.

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to verify MCP connection and get current tool schemas
Use IANA timezone identifiers like 'America/New_York' instead of abbreviations like 'EST'
Specify dates in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS) rather than natural language
Search for events by title first to get the event_id before updating or deleting
antiPatterns: Do not use natural language dates like 'tomorrow' or 'next week' in API calls
Do not set event_duration_minutes to 60 or higher; use event_duration_hour instead
Do not assume event titles can be used as identifiers; always search to get event_id
Do not use UTC timestamps (ending in 'Z') for local date queries without timezone offset

faq.question: Does this skill work without the Rube MCP server?
faq.answer: No, this skill requires the Rube MCP server to be connected. Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your configuration.

faq.question: Can I use natural language dates like 'tomorrow at 3pm'?
faq.answer: No, the Google Calendar API requires ISO 8601 format dates. You must convert 'tomorrow at 3pm' to '2026-02-26T15:00:00' with the appropriate timezone.

faq.question: How do I add attendees by name instead of email?
faq.answer: The Google Calendar API only accepts email addresses. Use a contact lookup tool like GMAIL_SEARCH_PEOPLE to resolve names to emails first.

faq.question: Why does my event show at the wrong time?
faq.answer: You may be using UTC timestamps (ending in 'Z') instead of local timezone offsets. Use RFC3339 format with offsets like '2026-02-26T15:00:00-08:00' for PST.

faq.question: What is the difference between PATCH_EVENT and UPDATE_EVENT?
faq.answer: PATCH_EVENT updates only specific fields while keeping existing values. UPDATE_EVENT replaces the entire event. Use PATCH for partial updates to avoid losing data.

faq.question: Can this skill create Google Meet links automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, Google Meet link creation is enabled by default. However, it may fail on personal Gmail accounts and will gracefully fall back to creating the event without a meet link.
